title: ficus hirta var. setosa
intro: Ficus hirta var. setosa, also known as the prickly fig or bengkudu, is a species of fig tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, thorny branches and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Ficus hirta var. setosa include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a juice, tea, or supplement, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions.
